title: Cannot start containers. OCI Runtime Error.
body:
**BUG REPORT**

**Description**

Consistent error - 
`Error: OCI runtime error: unable to start container "xxx": container_linux.go:380: starting container process caused: process_linux.go:393: copying bootstrap data to pipe caused: write init-p: broken pipe` 

This error causes the containers not to start, and as such also causes the systemd server to fail. 

This is on a fresh install (<24hrs) of Alma Linux 8.5 on physical hardware with the container features installed. I have a security profile enabled - [ACSC Essential Eight](https://www.redhat.com/en/resources/acsc-essential-eight-overview). 


**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Attempt to start a container or start  podman-restart.service

**Describe the results you received:**
Within a few seconds of attempting to start a container or the restart.service the error appears in journalctl. 


**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
One container (jellyfin) ran at first without issue, but cannot restart. 

Issue persists across restarts of the device. 

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.4.2
API Version:  3.4.2
Go Version:   go1.16.7
Built:        Wed Feb  2 22:32:09 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**


Not running latest version. 

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
Running on Physical Hardware.

comments.body: Please try if it works with crun instead.

I'm not sure if installing crun helped. But things now seem to be working even though I'd not tried anything after install crun? I went to start a container via CLI, and had to check the config on podman, and the existing container was running.

I'm normally starting/monitoring the containers via the cockpit podman applet. Does crun affect containers created & started by this method?

> I'm not sure if installing crun helped. But things now seem to be working even though I'd not tried anything after install crun?

I'm closing this issue since this is resolved. But feel free to re-open.

> Does crun affect containers created & started by this method?
@ted1922 In theory and documentation behavior of `crun` and `runc` is similar but there are instances where podman specific bugs are fixed and merged quickly into upstream crun.

But feel free to re-open this issue if something is unresolved.
